sessions.conversation_seed: Browsing Kyoto travelogues on Xiaohongshu reminded me of my solo trip there in 2018, the most confident period before my job transfer.
While learning Italian on Duolingo, I recalled the photo my father and I took at the Colosseum in Rome; the awe I felt for those ancient ruins remains unforgettable.

sessions.session_theme: Photography Gear and Solace in Solitude
sessions.conversation_seed: While organizing photos from my Sony A7C II, I saw the lanterns of Hoi An and suddenly remembered the cold medicine my mother sent when I was sick—a moment that felt both lonely and warm.
Scrolling through discount flights to the Norwegian fjords on a Friday night made Antarctica and Peru on my Bucket List feel within reach; the aurora Old K mentioned seems right before my eyes.
Checking train schedules during the Spring Festival reminded me of my father; later, taking my parents to Universal Studios Singapore healed my past confusion with their joyful expressions.
Zhang Wei's Excel itinerary for New Zealand and Switzerland was stunning, especially the details from Interlaken to Jungfrau; her ability to uncover hidden gems is worth learning.

sessions.session_theme: Jiangnan Getaways and Thai Night Markets
sessions.conversation_seed: After a tiring weekend exploring ancient towns in the Jiangnan region, I recalled the joy of eating fried kway teow with Lin Yue at the Chiang Mai night market; that vibrant atmosphere is something cafes just can't offer.

On my work anniversary, I missed the excitement of glacier trekking in Iceland with A Jie; it was my most liberating moment after transferring roles in 2021, and driving around Reykjavik felt wonderful.

sessions.session_theme: Singles' Day Shopping and Care from Abroad
sessions.conversation_seed: I bought a camera lens during the Singles' Day sale, but it reminded me of when I fell ill in Hoi An and Chen Xiulan specially delivered cold medicine and pickled vegetables to me—a friendship more precious than any gift.

Talking with Old K about the aurora in Norway, my mind drifted to Queenstown in New Zealand; the scenes of A Jie taking me skydiving and glacier trekking are vivid in my memory—that was true adventure.
Under Old K's guidance at the Uffizi Gallery in Florence, I fell in love with art; now, as Zhang Wei suggested, I collect fridge magnets and postcards on every trip as souvenirs.

Sorting through travel souvenirs gifted by friends sparked the inspiration to write a blog post about New Zealand and Iceland, while also reviewing how the lens I bought on Singles' Day performed under the aurora.
While city walking along the mural streets of George Town in Penang, I suddenly felt lonely and homesick, missing the coffee at a seaside guesthouse in Dali and Chen Xiulan's caring words.

sessions.session_theme: Paris Strolls and Longing for Snow-Capped Peaks
sessions.conversation_seed: The cafe in Montmartre recommended by Zhang Wei was fantastic; this kind of city walk is so relaxing, making me long again for the snow-capped mountains of Interlaken and the aurora of Reykjavik.

Recalling my solitary prayer at Fushimi Inari Taisha, that sense of tranquility stood in stark contrast to the lively debate Lin Yue, Zhang Wei, and I had about conflicting travel styles at the Chiang Mai night market.

When work stress mounts, Zhang Wei always mentions Kamakura or Kyoto; I reminisce about drinking coffee at a seaside guesthouse in Japan in 2018 and those hidden hole-in-the-wall eateries tucked away in alleys.
Planning to take my parents to Universal Studios Singapore next year; their happiness is what matters most. I remember buying some unique tableware as souvenirs last time, which they really loved.

sessions.session_theme: Snow Peaks, Auroras, and the Beauty of Data
sessions.conversation_seed: Looking at the Interlaken itinerary Zhang Wei planned in Excel, I marveled at how data can display beauty, just as shocking as snow peaks and auroras; next time I drive, I'll make sure to sit by the window.

sessions.session_theme: City of Art and Family Care
sessions.conversation_seed: The artistic atmosphere of Florence is intoxicating; it reminds me of the cold medicine my parents sent when I was in Rome. No matter how far I travel, family care remains the warmest backdrop.

sessions.session_theme: Reflecting on Role Transfer and the Spirit of Adventure
sessions.conversation_seed: At this mid-year review, reflecting on the changes since my role transfer in 2021, I thank the version of myself that trekked glaciers in Iceland and went skydiving in New Zealand; may I always maintain a mindset of excitement and celebration.
